start.title: 1 · Getting started
start.subtitle: Set up your organisation and get to know CompLens
start.step1Title: Register and set up your organisation
start.step1Body: After you register, CompLens creates your organisation automatically. Under Settings → Organisation you can adjust the name, country, and other metadata. This information appears on all reports and PDF exports.
start.step1Tip: Enter the full, correct organisation name — it appears automatically on your PDF reports and licence agreement.
start.step2Body: Under Settings → Team, administrators can invite colleagues (e.g. HR director, data protection officer, works council rep) by email. Choose a role: Admin (full access) or Viewer (read-only). Invitations are valid for seven days.
start.step2Tip: Your licence determines the number of user seats. Book additional seats as an add-on.
start.step3Body: Under Settings → Profile, enter your full name, job title, and preferred language (German/English). Your name and title appear on internal exports.
start.step4Body: Before importing data, download the official CSV template under Import → Download template. The template contains all mandatory fields required by EU Directive 2023/970 and the German Pay Transparency Act.
start.step4Tip: Always use the latest template — older versions may lack mandatory fields.
start.faq1Q: How long does the trial period last?
start.faq1A: The trial lasts seven days from registration. You have full access to all features. After it expires, access is suspended until you activate a licence. Trial reports carry a SAMPLE watermark.
start.faq2Q: Can I rename the organisation later?
start.faq2A: Yes. Under Settings → Organisation, administrators can change the name at any time. Previously generated PDF/PPT reports are not updated retrospectively.
start.faq3A: An overlay blocks dashboard interactions. Your data and analyses are retained. Click "Upgrade now" to proceed to the secure Stripe payment page.
start.faq4A: CompLens is designed for EU companies, particularly for the German and wider European market. Calculations are based on EU Directive 2023/970 and the German EntgTranspG.

import.title: 2 · Importing datasets
import.subtitle: Preparing and uploading CSV data correctly
import.step1Body: Open the CSV template in Excel or Google Sheets. Mandatory fields: employee_id (unique), gender (m/f/d), job_grade (pay grade), hourly_rate or annual_salary, department, employment_type (full_time/part_time/minijob), weekly_hours.
import.step1Tip: Use a semicolon (;) as the delimiter. CompLens accepts decimals with either a comma (25,50) or full stop (25.50).
import.step2Body: Optional fields: first_name, last_name (for right-to-information Art. 7), variable_pay (bonuses, premiums), overtime_hours, benefits_value (in-kind benefits in EUR/year). These improve the accuracy of total remuneration calculations.
import.step3Body: Open Import → New dataset. Enter a descriptive name and the reporting year, then upload your CSV. CompLens validates the data and flags any errors with row references.
import.step3Tip: The dataset name appears on your PDF cover page. Use something like "Pay data 2024 — Main company".
import.step4Body: If validation errors occur, CompLens lists them with affected row numbers. Common issues: missing gender values, invalid employment_type codes, negative hourly rates. Correct the CSV and re-upload.
import.step5Body: Archive datasets you no longer need (hidden but retained) or permanently delete them. A hard delete also removes all linked analyses and justifications (GDPR-compliant).
import.step5Tip: Prefer archiving: archived datasets remain available for trend analyses but stay hidden from the main selection.
import.faq1Q: What encoding must the CSV have?
import.faq1A: CompLens expects UTF-8 (with or without BOM). If umlauts display incorrectly, open the file in a text editor and re-save as UTF-8.
import.faq2Q: How do I enter part-time employees?
import.faq2A: Set employment_type to "part_time" and enter the contractually agreed hours in weekly_hours. CompLens normalises all salaries to gross hourly pay per Art. 3, Directive 2023/970.
import.faq3A: Mini-jobs (type "minijob") feed into the calculations but are reported separately, as they are statistically less meaningful. A separate cohort appears when there are five or more mini-job holders.
import.faq4Q: Should I include service providers or external staff?
import.faq4A: No. Under Directive 2023/970, only employees in the legal sense (permanent and part-time staff) are included — not self-employed persons, agency workers, or contractors.
import.faq5A: job_grade is your internal pay grade or remuneration level (e.g. "L1", "Senior", "Pay grade 5"). CompLens analyses the pay gap within each cohort. The more granular the classification, the more meaningful the result.

analysis.title: 3 · Analysis & results
analysis.subtitle: Understanding and interpreting pay gap calculations
analysis.step1Body: Go to Analysis → New analysis, select a dataset, and enter a name. Click "Start analysis". CompLens calculates the unadjusted and adjusted median and mean pay gap per the EU standard.
analysis.step1Tip: You can run multiple analyses from the same dataset — e.g. with different WIF weightings.
analysis.step2Title: Unadjusted vs. adjusted gender pay gap
analysis.step2Body: The unadjusted gap compares all women and men directly, without controlling for occupational group. The adjusted gap compares within the same cohort (job_grade) using WIF factors. Both values are legally reportable under Art. 9, Directive 2023/970.
analysis.step3Body: If the unadjusted median gap exceeds 5%, Art. 9(1)(c) requires action. CompLens flags this with a red status badge and generates a remediation plan proposal.
analysis.step3Tip: The 5% threshold applies to the unadjusted median gap. The adjusted gap shows what remains after controlling for occupational group and WIF factors.
analysis.step4Title: Results by department and pay grade
analysis.step4Body: The analysis detail view breaks results down by department and pay grade (job_grade). Cohorts with fewer than five people are anonymised and shown as "< 5 employees".
analysis.step5Body: The quartile view shows how women and men are distributed across each pay quartile. Art. 9(1)(b) requires companies to report this distribution.
analysis.faq1A: WIF stands for "Wage Influencing Factors" (from the German Wertigkeit, Inhalt, Funktion) — the objective criteria the EU Directive uses to assess work of equal value. By default, CompLens considers: qualification requirements, responsibility, workload, and working conditions.
analysis.faq2Q: Why does the median differ from the mean?
analysis.faq2A: The median is the middle value of a sorted list — more robust against outliers (e.g. top earners). The mean is pulled by outliers. The EU Directive primarily prescribes the median; CompLens shows both.
analysis.faq3Q: Why are cohorts with fewer than five people hidden?
analysis.faq3A: Cohorts with fewer than five people of a gender group are not broken down, per GDPR Art. 5(1)(c) (data minimisation). They still feed into the overall gap.
analysis.faq4A: The EU Directive requires you to retain reporting data for at least four years. CompLens stores analyses permanently until you delete them. For long-term archiving, we also recommend the PDF export.

explanations.title: 4 · Recording justifications
explanations.subtitle: Documenting and justifying pay differences
explanations.step1Body: Justifications let you explain statistically notable pay gaps within a cohort using objective, gender-neutral factors — for example, performance reviews, length of service, or market rates for specialists.
explanations.step1Tip: Justifications reduce the adjusted gap but never the unadjusted gap. Both are reportable.
explanations.step2Body: In the analysis detail view, click "Add justification" for a cohort. Select a category (Performance, Market, Tenure, etc.), enter the share as a percentage or absolute hourly value, and write a description.
explanations.step3Body: All justifications appear automatically in the PDF report (page 3: adjustment overview) and the PPT presentation. The report shows what proportion of the total gap is justified.
explanations.step3Tip: Write justifications clearly and factually — they may serve as evidence during an audit or regulatory enquiry.
explanations.step4Body: Edit existing justifications via the edit icon (✎) or delete them. Changes take immediate effect on the adjusted gap.
explanations.faq1Q: How many justifications can I record per cohort?
explanations.faq1A: There is no limit. You can combine multiple factors (e.g. 2% tenure + 1.5% performance). CompLens sums all justification shares and shows the remaining unexplained portion.
explanations.faq2A: Justifications are your internal documentation. They do not replace a legal review. However, they serve as supporting evidence if authorities or courts enquire. Have critical justifications reviewed by a specialist employment lawyer.
explanations.faq3Q: What is the difference between "explained" and "unexplained"?
explanations.faq3A: "Explained" means the gap is backed by a documented, objective justification. "Unexplained" means the remaining gap has no recorded justification and is legally considered potential discrimination.

remediationModule.title: 5 · Remediation plans
remediationModule.subtitle: Closing pay gaps with structured plans
remediationModule.step1Body: Go to Remediation → New plan, select the affected analysis, and enter a title and target date. A plan can contain multiple steps, each assigned to a cohort or department.
remediationModule.step2Body: Each step includes: title, description, assignment (department/cohort), responsible person, and target date. Mark steps as "open", "in progress", or "completed".
remediationModule.step2Tip: Be specific: define measurable targets per step, e.g. "3% salary increase for women in L3 by 31 Dec 2025".
remediationModule.step3Body: The remediation dashboard shows all active plans with progress bars, open steps, and overdue actions. The overall indicator aggregates progress across all plans.
remediationModule.faq1A: Yes. The remediation plan is integrated into the PDF report (from page 4 onwards). A standalone export function for remediation plans will be provided in a future update.
remediationModule.faq2Q: Does a remediation plan change my analysis?
remediationModule.faq2A: No. Remediation plans are purely documentary and do not affect any calculation results. For an updated analysis, upload a new dataset and run a new analysis.

reports.title: 6 · Reports & exports
reports.subtitle: Creating PDF reports and PPT presentations
reports.step1Body: On the analysis detail page, click "Export PDF". The report includes: cover page with your organisation name, executive summary, pay gap overview, cohort details, quartile distribution, justification overview, and remediation plan (if available).
reports.step1Tip: Trial or expired-licence reports carry a red SAMPLE watermark and are limited to four pages.
reports.step2Body: Click "Export PPT". The presentation has seven slides: cover, executive summary, pay gap overview, departments, quartiles, salary comparison, and methodology. Ideal for board or works council presentations.
reports.step3Body: All exports display your organisation name prominently. CompLens appears only as a small "Created with CompLens" note — so your reports look fully in-house.
reports.step4Body: Under Settings → Subscription, licensed users can download the personalised software licence agreement (pre-signed by DexterBee GmbH) as a PDF.
reports.faq1Q: How many pages does the PDF report have?
reports.faq1A: A full report with an active licence runs 6–10 pages depending on data volume. During the trial, pages 5 onwards are replaced by an upgrade page.
reports.faq2Q: Can I submit the report directly to the authorities?
reports.faq2A: CompLens reports serve as internal documentation and analysis. Exact submission requirements (anti-discrimination bodies, employer associations) vary by company size and country. Consult a specialist employment lawyer for the official filing.
reports.faq3Q: In which languages are reports generated?
reports.faq3A: Currently, all reports are generated in German (mandatory language under EntgTranspG for German companies). Multilingual exports are planned for future versions.

portal.title: 7 · Right to information (Art. 7)
portal.subtitle: Providing individual employee pay information
portal.step1Title: What is the right to information?
portal.step1Body: Art. 7, Directive 2023/970 requires employers to provide employees, on request, with information about their own pay compared to the average pay of their peer group. You must respond within two months.
portal.step2Body: Under Right to Information → Employee search, enter the name or personnel ID of the requesting person (at least three characters). CompLens shows the peer-group pay information.
portal.step2Tip: Search only works for employees who are recorded with first_name/last_name in the imported dataset.
portal.step3Body: Click "Generate PDF / Print". CompLens produces a professional disclosure letter with your organisation name, the legally required comparative data, and the legal basis reference (Art. 7, Directive 2023/970). Print it directly or send by email.
portal.faq1A: Every employee may request this information once per year. The disclosure includes the peer-group median, broken down by gender — not individual colleagues’ salaries.
portal.faq2Q: How do I protect other employees’ anonymity?
portal.faq2A: CompLens shows only medians and means per cohort, never individual salaries. Cohorts with fewer than five people are suppressed. The letter contains no personal data of third parties.
portal.faq3Q: What if the peer group is too small?
portal.faq3A: If the employee’s cohort has fewer than five people, CompLens cannot provide the information. You’ll need to define an alternative peer group — contact support for this special case.

bands.title: 8 · Pay bands (Art. 9)
bands.subtitle: Creating internal pay bands and checking EU intra-grade compliance
bands.step1Body: Pay bands define the minimum and maximum pay per pay grade. Art. 9 requires companies to publish pay information by category and gender. An intra-grade gap of 5% or more triggers the justification obligation under Art. 10.
bands.step1Tip: Compa-ratio = (median salary / band midpoint) x 100. Values below 87% are a typical warning sign for underpayment in C&B practice.
bands.step2Body: CompLens detects the job_grade values from your data and suggests the right naming scheme (G scale, L scale, TVoD, TV-L, ERA, E scale, etc.). All pay grades are created and statistics calculated in one click.
bands.step2Tip: You can adjust the naming scheme at any time under Settings → Pay bands.
bands.step3Body: For each pay grade, CompLens calculates: Min, P25, Median, P75, Max (base salary), separate female/male medians, headcount (n), intra-grade gap, and total remuneration median. The Art. 9 heatmap rates each grade: green = compliant (< 5%), red = action required (>= 5%).
bands.step4Body: Optionally enter external market data (e.g. Kienbaum, Radford, StepStone) per grade — source, year, and external P25/P50/P75. These appear in the box-plot chart as a comparison band.
bands.step5Body: Band visualisations appear in the "Pay bands (Art. 9)" tab, on the dashboard (seventh KPI card), as an optional PDF page (toggle in PDF options), and as a dedicated PPT slide with an IQR chart.
bands.faq1Q: Intra-grade gap vs. overall gender pay gap — what is the difference?
bands.faq1A: The overall gap compares all women and men company-wide. The intra-grade gap (Art. 9) compares them within the same pay grade. Both are reportable; the intra-grade gap is harder to justify because the cohort is already comparable.
bands.faq2Q: What happens with grades with fewer than five people?
bands.faq2A: Pay grades with fewer than five people of a gender group are anonymised. They appear grey in the heatmap.
bands.faq3Q: Do I have to define pay bands manually?
bands.faq3A: No. CompLens creates bands automatically from your imported job_grade values. You can adjust min/max boundaries afterwards. Manual overrides are possible at any time.

trendsModule.title: 9 · Trend analysis
trendsModule.subtitle: Comparing pay gap development over multiple years
trendsModule.step1Body: Open Trend Analysis. In the dataset selection area, tick at least two datasets to compare. CompLens pre-selects a sensible default based on reporting year.
trendsModule.step1Tip: You need at least two datasets for trend lines and delta cards.
trendsModule.step2Body: Default mode shows one line per reporting year (one dataset per year). Comparison mode (toggle top-right) shows each dataset as its own line — even when multiple datasets cover the same year. Ideal for A/B scenarios (e.g. with vs. without a department).
trendsModule.step2Tip: Use comparison mode to contrast scenarios, e.g. "Dataset 2024 total" vs. "Dataset 2024 excl. management".
trendsModule.step3Body: Each delta card shows the current value and the year-on-year change. Green = improvement (gap decreased), red = deterioration (gap increased). The comparison always refers to the immediately preceding year in the selection.
trendsModule.step4Body: The Departments and Grades tabs colour-code the adjusted pay gap per department/grade and year. Dark red = gap > 10%, orange = 5–10%, yellow = 2–5%, green = < 2%. Ideal for board or works council progress reports.
trendsModule.faq1Q: Why can’t I see a trend view?
trendsModule.faq1A: You must first select at least 2 datasets/analyses in the dataset selection. Without a selection the message “Please select at least one dataset” is shown.
trendsModule.faq2Q: Can I add the same dataset twice?
trendsModule.faq2A: No. Each dataset can only be selected once. If you wish to compare scenarios, import the same dataset with different parameters (e.g. filtered employee groups).
trendsModule.faq3Q: Are trends also shown in the report?
trendsModule.faq3A: Trend charts are currently only available in the interactive trend view. Integration into the PDF report is planned for a future version.

compliance.title: 10 · GDPR & compliance
compliance.subtitle: Data protection, DPA and legal evidence
compliance.step1Body: If you process personal data (salaries, names) in CompLens, Art. 28 GDPR requires a DPA with DexterBee GmbH. Request one via the button on the Compliance page.
compliance.step1Tip: The DPA is free. We send it by email within two working days.
compliance.step2Body: We process all data in the EU (Frankfurt am Main). Transfers use TLS 1.3 encryption; data at rest uses AES-256. CompLens accesses Google Gemini via EU API endpoints — your data is never used for model training.
compliance.step3Body: Delete individual datasets, analyses, or your entire account at any time. A hard delete irreversibly removes all linked data. On request, we create a complete data export before deletion.
compliance.faq1Q: Who is the controller under the GDPR?
compliance.faq1A: Your organisation is the controller (Art. 4(7) GDPR). DexterBee GmbH is the processor (Art. 4(8) GDPR). The DPA governs this relationship.
compliance.faq2Q: Is our data used for anything else?
compliance.faq2A: No. We use your pay data solely to provide the CompLens service. No disclosure to third parties, no model training. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR (performance of a contract).

settings.title: 11 · Settings & team
settings.subtitle: Managing profile, organisation and subscription
settings.step1Body: Under Settings → Profile, set your name, job title, and preferred language (German/English). These settings are per-user and don't affect other team members.
settings.step2Body: CompLens supports German and English. Select your language in the Profile tab — it takes effect immediately across the entire interface. Reports are generated in German (legal requirement for German companies).
settings.step2Tip: Admins can set the language individually for each team member.
settings.step3Body: The Subscription tab shows your current plan, expiry date, and seat usage. Licensed users can download the pre-filled software licence agreement as a PDF. Stripe checkout links are available for upgrades and add-ons.
settings.step4Body: Admin: full access — import, analysis, settings, and team management. Viewer: read-only access to analyses, reports, and the portal. Only admins can change roles.
settings.faq1A: Yes. On the sign-in page, click "Forgot password?". We’ll email you a reset link (valid for 60 minutes).
settings.faq2Q: How do I cancel my subscription?
settings.faq2A: Submit a support ticket or write to us with three months’ notice to the end of the year. Contact our support team for cancellation queries.
settings.faq3Q: What happens to my data after cancellation?
settings.faq3A: You have 30 days after the contract ends to export your data (analyses, reports). After that, all data is irrevocably deleted. On request, we create a full export beforehand.

jobArchitecture.title: Job Architecture
jobArchitecture.subtitle: Job families, leveling, competencies & grade mapping
jobArchitecture.step1Body: Go to Job Architecture → Leveling. Choose from the CompLens Standard template (L1-L10), create with the CompLens Assistant, or build manually. You can have multiple structures and switch the default at any time.
jobArchitecture.step1Tip: The CompLens Assistant can generate a custom structure based on your industry and company size.
jobArchitecture.step2Body: Go to Families & Jobs. Create families like Engineering, Finance, or HR to group related positions. You can also initialise 8 default families with one click.
jobArchitecture.step3Body: Within each family, create jobs with a title and level. Add a job description (summary, responsibilities, qualifications) — or let the CompLens Assistant generate one.
jobArchitecture.step3Tip: The assistant also suggests matching competencies from your library.
jobArchitecture.step4Body: Go to Competencies. Create competencies with a 5-level proficiency scale. Categories: Core, Leadership, Technical, Functional. Link competencies to jobs with required levels and importance ratings.
jobArchitecture.step5Body: Go to Grade Mapping. Map each level in your structure to a pay band grade. This connects your job architecture to your compensation framework.
jobArchitecture.step6Title: Upload job descriptions for level mapping
jobArchitecture.step6Body: Go to JD Upload. Upload a PDF or Word file containing a job description. The CompLens Assistant analyses it and suggests the appropriate level with confidence and reasoning.
jobArchitecture.step6Tip: You can accept the suggestion and create a job directly from the result.
jobArchitecture.faq1Q: What leveling structure should I choose?
jobArchitecture.faq1A: If you don't have an existing grading system, start with the CompLens Standard (L1-L10). If you have a specific system (TVöD, HAY, Mercer IPE, etc.), use the assistant to generate a matching structure.
jobArchitecture.faq2Q: Can I change my leveling structure later?
jobArchitecture.faq2A: Yes. You can add, remove, or reorder levels at any time. You can also create additional structures and switch the default.
jobArchitecture.faq3Q: Is job architecture required for pay gap analysis?
jobArchitecture.faq3A: No. Pay gap analysis works independently with employee data. Job architecture is an optional add-on for organisations that want structured job frameworks.
jobArchitecture.faq4Q: How are jobs linked to salary bands?
jobArchitecture.faq4A: Via Grade Mapping. Each level in your structure can be mapped to a grade in your salary bands. When you create a job, the matching salary range is shown automatically.
jobArchitecture.faq5Q: What does the CompLens Assistant generate?
jobArchitecture.faq5A: The assistant can generate leveling structures, job descriptions, competency descriptions, behavioural indicators, and level suggestions from uploaded JDs. All suggestions can be reviewed and adjusted before saving.

prompt.role: You are an expert in EU pay transparency (Directive 2023/970 / EntgTranspG).
prompt.instruction: HR has already defined a concrete compensation plan. Create a written remediation plan based EXCLUSIVELY on these defined steps. Do not invent your own figures.
prompt.targetSalary: Recommended target salary (closes residual gap only)
prompt.planTitle: HR-DEFINED COMPENSATION PLAN (BASIS FOR THE REMEDIATION PLAN)
prompt.useOnlySteps: IMPORTANT: Use EXCLUSIVELY these steps and their figures. The plan has already been decided — formulate it as a written remediation plan.
prompt.focusResidual: Also focus on the remaining residual gap of {residualPct}%. The {explainedPct}% explained by HR justification do NOT need to be adjusted.
prompt.outputInstructions:
Create a remediation plan with these sections (English, factual, direct):
1. **Action required** — Raw gap, explained portion, residual gap (state 3 figures clearly)
2. **Planned measures** — Describe each defined step with time horizon, concrete EUR amounts (base + variable) and final state
3. **Target salary** — Final state after all steps (base + variable + total), comparison to cohort median
4. **Timeline** — Timeline of the defined steps (horizons from plan)
5. **Responsibility** — Responsible persons from the steps
6. **Risk of inaction** — legal / reputational

Max. 380 words. No marketing language. Direct and professional.

compliance.heading: GDPR & Compliance
compliance.subheading: Data protection, security & EU AI Act — As of: March 2026 ·
compliance.summaryBanner: CompLens processes compensation data exclusively on servers within the European Union. Data never leaves the EU at any point. Operations are carried out by DexterBee GmbH (HRB 17694, Local Court Aschaffenburg) as a sub-processor pursuant to Art. 28 GDPR. Managing Director: Stephan Dongjin Oh · VAT ID: DE369096037

infrastructure.title: Server Infrastructure & Data Residency
infrastructure.intro: All components of the CompLens platform are operated exclusively within the European Union. No data transfer to third countries takes place.
infrastructure.dbDetail: All database records, user profiles and uploaded files are physically stored in the AWS data centre in Frankfurt. Row Level Security (RLS) ensures technical tenant isolation.
infrastructure.frontendDetail: Serverless Functions are strictly executed in the fra1 region. SOC 2 Type II certified.
infrastructure.aiDetail: AI requests are routed through certified EU API endpoints. Customer data is not used for AI training (no data opt-in).
infrastructure.emailDetail: System notifications (invitations, password resets). GDPR compliance via EU-US DPF and DPA.

toms.title: Technical & Organisational Measures (TOMs)
toms.summary: CompLens implements comprehensive security measures per GDPR Art. 32 — including TLS 1.3, AES-256 encryption, Row Level Security, pseudonymisation, and a documented deletion policy.
toms.transitDetail: TLS 1.3 for all connections between client, CDN and API routes. Older protocols (TLS 1.0/1.1) are disabled.
toms.restDetail: AES-256 encryption at disk level in AWS eu-central-1 for all databases, backups and file storage.
toms.rlsDetail: Every database row is linked to an org_id. PostgreSQL RLS policies ensure that no organisation can access another organisation’s data — even in the event of programming errors.
toms.accessDetail: Authentication via Supabase Auth (Magic Link / email + password, bcrypt hashing). Role-based permission model: Admin / HR Analyst / Viewer.
toms.zeroPiiDetail: No real names or personal identifiers are transmitted in AI requests. The AI exclusively receives pseudonymised identifiers and factual compensation data.
toms.deletionDetail: User accounts and all linked organisation data can be irreversibly deleted at any time (hard delete with cascading removal of all analyses and reports). After contract termination: deletion within 30 days.
toms.auditsDetail: Supabase and Vercel are SOC 2 Type II certified. Google Cloud is ISO 27001 and SOC 2 certified.
toms.fullTomsLink: View full TOMs pursuant to Art. 32 GDPR

aiAct.title: EU AI Act Compliance (Generative AI)
aiAct.banner: CompLens uses Generative AI (Google Gemini 2.5 Pro) as an assistive system. The AI falls into the “low risk” category under the EU AI Act classification (not a high-risk system per Annex III).
aiAct.rolesDetail: Under the EU AI Act, DexterBee GmbH is the deployer of the AI system. Google is the provider of the foundation model. Responsibilities are clearly defined pursuant to Art. 28 AI Act.
aiAct.humanDetail: CompLens does not make autonomous decisions about employees. AI-generated texts (justifications, action plans) must be manually reviewed and approved by HR managers.
aiAct.transparencyDetail: At every point in the system it is clearly visible when content has been generated by AI. There are no hidden AI outputs.
aiAct.noTrainingLabel: No AI Training with Customer Data
aiAct.noTrainingDetail: Google processes requests via EU API endpoints without storing data for training purposes (Data Processing Addendum active).
aiAct.minimisationDetail: AI prompts exclusively contain pseudonymised employee IDs and factual compensation information. Real names, emails and direct personal identifiers are never transmitted to AI APIs.

directive.title: EU Directive 2023/970/EU — Pay Transparency
directive.intro: CompLens was specifically designed to comply with the EU Pay Transparency Directive and the German Pay Transparency Act (EntgTranspG). Calculations and report exports were validated in collaboration with HR and data protection professionals.
directive.item1: Calculation of unadjusted and adjusted gender pay gap per Art. 9 of the Directive
directive.item2: Output of median and mean pay gap per pay component
directive.item3: Automatic warning when the 5% threshold is exceeded (Art. 10) with an action plan
directive.item4: EU-compliant reports (PDF and PowerPoint) for submission to authorities
directive.item5: GDPR-compliant right-to-information module for individual employee requests (§ 10 EntgTranspG)
directive.item6: Support for employee representatives with differentiated read permissions

subprocessors.intro: All sub-processors have been reviewed for EU data protection compliance and have committed to the strictest European standards through DPAs or SCCs.

adLanding.faq1Question: Does this apply to my company?
adLanding.faq1Answer: The EU Pay Transparency Directive applies to all EU employers. Companies with 150+ employees must report by June 2027 (based on 2026 data). Companies with 100+ employees must report by June 2031. Even smaller organisations benefit from proactive compliance.
adLanding.faq2Answer: A CSV or Excel export from your HRIS containing employee records with job title, department, gender, and compensation data. CompLens works with SAP, Workday, Personio, and any system that can export structured data.
adLanding.faq3Answer: Absolutely. All data is processed on EU servers (Frankfurt), encrypted in transit and at rest, and handled in full GDPR compliance. We never share your data with third parties. Deletion on request.
adLanding.faq4Question: How long does the analysis take?
adLanding.faq4Answer: The initial readiness check takes under 2 minutes. A full pay gap analysis with report generation typically completes in under 5 minutes, regardless of company size.
adLanding.faq5Answer: Yes. Every account starts with a 14-day full-feature trial — no credit card required. Exports include a SAMPLE watermark during the trial.
adLanding.faq6Answer: CompLens includes built-in remediation planning. You get actionable recommendations for closing the gap, priority-ranked by impact, with cost projections and timeline estimates.
